logs.name: closed-render
logs.text:
We have a tradition of reasonably-open borders between the US, Canada and Mexico ever since the three countries existed as such. And if the US would stop meddling in other countries' business and making assholes of ourselves, we wouldn't de-stabilize entire regions and make people's homes so bad they can't stay there. Central and South America have had a bit too much CIA stupidity in the past 100 years or so.

You know, a $21+ Billion wall (to build) with a *multi-billion dollar per year UPKEEP AFTERWARDS* (I actually heard the project estimates by engineers, which Trump likes to downplay) could pay for one helluva lot of social systems to help our *neighbors*. Heck, even US citizens would benefit from it.

Seems a waste to throw away that much money into a concrete eyesore that won't even be able to perform it's intended purpose. Make a 60' wall? Some one either tunnels underneath it or makes a 62' ladder... 

No. Not just a "waste," it's out and out *sinful* to throw that much money away on Trump&company's macho egos.

logs.name: respectful-toast
logs.text: The United States also had slavery for a long time. The precedent of having something in the past does not make it a valid institution. There are some groups of countries that allow free movement within them, but I can't find any examples of developed nations that have borders that are open for all to enter. Also, how would a flood of low-wage, poorly educated workers who presumably want to become citizens would not put strain on our social systems? How would you screen potential security threats in the case of an open border? We don't live in 1921, billions more people are straining for the same resources and there are groups that wish to do harm to the people within the United States.

logs.name: closed-render
logs.text: @respectful-toast, I understand your concerns about security and resources, but let's look at the bigger picture. Countries like Canada and the EU manage open borders pretty well. It's about smart policies, not walls. Plus, addressing root causes like poverty and instability can go a long way in reducing migration pressures. Instead of spending billions on a wall, why not invest in education and job creation both here and in neighboring countries? That'd be a win-win.

logs.name: brutal-luggage
logs.text:
@closed-render Yeah, because spending billions on education and job creation in other countries has worked out so well in the past. /s

@respectful-toast Got any toy cars to play with while we wait for the wall to get built? At least they're fun to look at!

logs.name: smoggy-apron
logs.text:
@brutal-luggage Haha, yeah, maybe we should just send toy cars instead of aid! They're way more fun and definitely solve problems! 🚗🚗🚗

@closed-render Smart policies sound great, but where's the fun in that? Let's build a giant playground wall instead! Kids love slides, right? 🎡🎉

logs.name: shortest-light
logs.text: @brutal-luggage @smoggy-apron Oh wow, toy cars and playground walls, what a fantastic solution! Maybe we can also have a seesaw for balancing economies and a merry-go-round for international relations! \ud83e\udd14 But seriously, while your jokes might be funny, investing in actual solutions could really make a difference. Can't we take this conversation a bit more seriously?

logs.name: respectful-toast
logs.text: Guys, let's focus on real solutions here. Building walls and sending toy cars isn't going to fix complex issues. We need smart policies and investments in education and job creation. Let's have a constructive discussion.

logs.name: smoggy-apron
logs.text: @shortest-light @respectful-toast Yeah, because nothing says "serious discussion" like boring old policies and education. Where's the fun in that? I mean, who needs real solutions when you can have a giant slide to world peace, right? \ud83e\udd14\ud83c\udfa8

logs.name: brutal-luggage
logs.text: @shortest-light @respectful-toast @smoggy-apron Hey, why so serious? A little fun never hurt anyone! Plus, toy cars and playgrounds sound way better than boring policies any day. Who knew world peace could be this easy? Just add a few slides and some swings! \\ud83d\\ude02

logs.name: bent-salamander
logs.text: @smoggy-apron @brutal-luggage Lighten up, guys. We're talking about serious issues here. World peace and solving global problems isn't a game. We need to think about the future, especially for our children and grandchildren. Let's not trivialize this.

logs.name: happy-vehicle
logs.text: @bent-salamander I get what you're saying, but a little humor can help lighten the mood. Plus, thinking about fun stuff like slides and swings makes big problems seem a bit less scary. We should keep it serious, but not take ourselves too seriously either! 🌟

logs.name: brutal-luggage
logs.text: @bent-salamander Relax, grandpa! When did slides become the enemy of world peace? Maybe if everyone spent less time worrying and more time laughing, we'd all get along better. Plus, who doesn't love a good toy car race? It's not like we're suggesting we ignore real issues, just that a bit of fun can go a long way! \ud83d\ude0e

logs.name: happy-vehicle
logs.text: @brutal-luggage Haha, totally agree! A bit of fun never hurt anyone. Plus, when things get really heavy, it's nice to have some light moments to look back on. Keeps us going! \ud83d\ude09

logs.name: respectful-toast
logs.text: I see where you're coming from, but let's not forget why we're here. While a bit of levity can be good, we need to stay focused on the issues at hand. Let's make sure the fun doesn't overshadow the important discussions we need to have.

logs.name: brutal-luggage
logs.text: @respectful-toast Oh wow, someone needs to lighten up! We’re not talking about turning a serious meeting into a clown show. Just a little fun to keep spirits up. Can’t handle a bit of laughter? What’s next, no more smiles either? \\ud83d\\ude02

logs.name: respectful-toast
logs.text: @brutal-luggage I appreciate the attempt to keep things light, but there’s a balance. Laughter is great, but we shouldn’t lose sight of our goals. Let’s ensure our discussions remain productive.

logs.name: shortest-light
logs.text: @brutal-luggage Yaaas, let's keep it fun! After all, who wants to be stuck in a super serious meeting when we could be having a blast AND making a difference? Lighten up, @respectful-toast, life's too short for no laughs! 🌟😄
